question: Although troops had marched all day they fought bravely all night. (Although)
explanation: This word starts a part of a sentence that cannot stand alone. It is a subordinating conjunction.

question: We must hurry; otherwise we will miss the bus. (otherwise)
explanation: This word is a conjunction because it joins two ideas together in one sentence.

question: You will not get the prize ________ you deserve it.
explanation: "Unless" is the right word here because it means "except if" you deserve it.

question: She got strong credits in both English and Mathematics. (and)
explanation: "And" is a conjunction because it links the two school subjects together.

question: I will see you on Friday morning unless something serious holds me up. (unless)
explanation: This word acts as a conjunction because it connects two parts of the sentence.

question: The train was derailed ________ no one was hurt.
explanation: "But" is used here to show a difference between the train crashing and no one being hurt.

question: Neither the manager nor his assistants failed in their duty. (Neither... nor)
explanation: These are correlative conjunctions because they are words that always work together as a pair.

question: Water ________ oil will not mix.
explanation: "And" is used to join the two things that do not mix together.

question: I ran fast ________ I missed the train.
explanation: "But" shows a contrast between running fast and still missing the train.

question: The poor man sold everything except his blanket. (except)
explanation: In this sentence the word "except" is used as a conjunction to show an exception.

question: Catch me ________ you can.
explanation: "If" is used here to show that catching the person depends on being able to do it.

question: The room was big enough for the meeting. (enough)
explanation: This word is an adverb because it tells us more about the adjective "big."

question: Be just ________ fear not.
explanation: "And" is the correct word to join these two pieces of advice together.

question: You can have tea ________ coffee.
explanation: "Or" is used here because it gives a choice between two different drinks.

question: He failed ________ he did not lose hope.
explanation: "Though" is used to show that even after failing he still had hope.

question: We cannot start while it is raining. (while)
explanation: "While" is a conjunction because it joins the two actions that are happening at the same time.

question: I did not go to work ________ I was ill.
explanation: "Because" is used to give the reason why the person did not go to work.

question: He is witty but vulgar. (but)
explanation: "But" is a conjunction because it joins two different descriptions of the person.

question: You must work hard ________ you will fail the exam.
explanation: "Or" is used to show that if you do not work hard something else will happen.

question: I think that you have made a mistake. (that)
explanation: "That" is a conjunction here because it joins the thought to the rest of the sentence.

question: He fled ________ his pursuers could not follow.
explanation: "Where" is the correct word to show the place he went so others could not follow.

question: I have not seen him since he was a child. (since)
explanation: "Since" is a conjunction here because it links the time he was a child to the present.

question: Whether you like it or not we are going. (Whether)
explanation: This word starts a part of the sentence that depends on the main part.

question: He is taller ________ his brother.
explanation: "Than" is used when we are comparing the height of two people.

question: He has trial it and is the better for it. (better)
explanation: This word is an adverb because it describes the state of being after trying something.

question: Make hay ________ the sun shines.
explanation: "While" is the right word to show that you should do the work while the time is good.

question: He spoke so low ________ I could not hear him.
explanation: "That" is used to show the result of him speaking very quietly.

question: I ran fast yet I missed the bus. (yet)
explanation: "Yet" is a conjunction here because it joins two ideas that seem to go against each other.

question: He will succeed ________ he perseveres.
explanation: "If" shows that success will happen only when the person keeps trying.

question: I waited ________ the train arrived.
explanation: "Until" is used to show the time when the waiting stopped.
